name: Tag Page

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
\### Fixed
- Resolve relative image embeds (`![[...]]` and `![](...)`) when rendering `tag-page` output so images render correctly from code block output.
- Only the tagged source bullet gets a reference link; child/sub-bullets no longer get links appended.

### Changed
- Use the current `MarkdownRenderer.render` API for rendering tag-page content.

### Internal
- Carry the source note path through tag match metadata to enable embed resolution.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes

## New
- Auto‑migration: when a legacy tag page is opened, the plugin inserts a `tag-page` block and a legacy marker.
- New command: **Clean up legacy tag page content** removes everything below the migration marker.
- Configurable **Legacy frontmatter key** for detecting old pages.

## Notes
- This release focuses only on migration utilities; the `tag-page` code block rendering was released previously.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes

## 🚨 Breaking Changes

- **Tag pages are now rendered exclusively via the `tag-page` code block.**  
  Existing pages that relied on automatic full-page replacement must add a `tag-page` block to render content.

- **`frontmatterQueryProperty` / `tag-page-query` has been removed.**  
  Any workflows depending on frontmatter-based refresh must switch to using the code block.

- **Tag page detection has changed.**  
  Tag pages are now excluded from tag scanning by folder (`tagPageDir`) instead of frontmatter, which changes how “tag pages” are identified.

## ✨ New

- **Markdown code block support**
~~~
```tag-page
tags: #this #that
```
~~~

This allows you to keep your own content above and/or below the rendered results.

- **Create Tag Page** now seeds a page with the `tag-page` block (no pre-rendered content).

- **Create Tag Page modal** includes a tag suggestion dropdown as you type.

- **New setting: Link position**  
Choose whether reference links appear at the start or end of each pulled line.


## 🛠 Fixed / Improved

- **Wildcard tags now match nested levels**  
Example: `#example/*` matches `#example/tag/nested`.

- **Improved tag detection**  
Tags now correctly match punctuation and end-of-line cases (e.g. `#foo.` matches `#foo`).

- **Cleaned up legacy paths**  
Removed old frontmatter and auto-refresh logic that is no longer needed.

## 🔁 Migration Notes

- For each existing tag page:
   - Insert the new `tag-page` code block.
   - Remove the old generated list.
   - Personal notes can remain above or below the block.
   - Old frontmatter keys can be ignored or deleted; rendering no longer depends on them.
   - If you used custom title templates, note that the rendered title now appears *inside* the block output.

## Closes
- #22 
- #31
- #37 
- #41 
- #42 
- #50

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## TagPagePlugin Alpha Release v0.0.1

Announcing the initial alpha release of the TagPagePlugin for Obsidian. This plugin enhances your Obsidian experience by offering dynamic, updatedable pages for your tags. Tag pages can pull in all references to a specific tag and display them neatly within a single note, offering you a consolidated view of that tag's presence across your vault.

### Features

- **Tag Page Directory**: Automatically create tag pages in a specific directory, customizable via settings.
- **Frontmatter Query Property**: Utilizes a frontmatter property to store queries in the tag page.
- **Bulleted Sub-Items**: Option to include bulleted sub-items in the tag page.
- **Include Lines**: Option to include entire lines where the tag appears.

### Quick Commands

- `Refresh Tag Page`: Manually refresh the tag page content via a ribbon icon.
- `Create Tag Page`: Create a new tag page or navigate to an existing one via command palette.

### Installation

1. Download the `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` files from this release.
2. Place them in your `.obsidian/plugins/tag-page-md/` directory.

### How to Use

1. Navigate to the Plugin Options within Obsidian and configure the settings as per your needs.
2. Use the `Create Tag Page` command or click the ribbon icon to create or refresh tag pages.

### Changelog

- Initial alpha release.

### Known Issues

- This being an alpha release, some minor bugs and incomplete features are expected.

### Contributing

Feel free to contribute to this project by opening issues, suggesting improvements, or submitting pull requests.
